title: Executive Order 10277—Amending Regulations Relating to the Safeguarding of Vessels, Harbors, Ports, and Waterfront Facilities of the United States

flags.justification: Amends §6.10-1 and §6.10-7 to flip the eligibility presumption: the original EO 10173 denied employment only if the Commandant affirmatively found a person inimical to security; this amendment denies employment unless the Commandant is affirmatively satisfied the person is NOT inimical — a meaningful expansion of the Commandant's practical gatekeeping power, shifting the burden onto the individual. The pre-existing appeal mechanism is preserved, which keeps this at PRESENT rather than CRITICAL.
The §6.10-9 appeal Board (Coast Guard, management, and labor representation) is unchanged and preserved by this amendment.
Single coherent purpose: amending the existing port security regulations.
The underlying 'character and habits of life' standard remains as undefined as in EO 10173, now more consequential because ambiguity favors exclusion under the flipped presumption.
No incentive concern distinct from the vagueness already captured under Flag 4.
The underlying open-ended, threat-based regulatory regime (EO 10173) is unchanged in this respect by the amendment.
No existing oversight is removed; the new Subpart 6.19 clarifies rather than displaces responsibility, and the appeal mechanism persists unchanged.
The same labor/management-inclusive appeal Board from EO 10173 persists unchanged.

The burden-of-proof flip is a genuinely significant precedent — moving from a presumption of eligibility (government must justify exclusion) to a presumption of exclusion (individual must prove eligibility) for an entire occupational category on security grounds, mirroring the guilt-presumption pattern that became prominent in Cold War-era loyalty-security screening.
The same agency (Coast Guard) amends its own existing rules; no other agency's mandate is implicated.
The Commandant's unbounded category-exemption power from EO 10173 (§6.10-1) persists unchanged in this amendment.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 5/22 = 22.7% — numerically identical to EO 10173, but the underlying substance shifted meaningfully: this amendment reverses the burden of proof governing an entire occupational category's employment eligibility. Second of three related port-security orders in this batch (see EO 10173, EO 10352).
